Hi, and welcome back to Line Sheet, live from Milan, where Sabato De Sarno wants you to know he is not slinking away. The former Gucci designer was out and about this week as anticipation builds about the impending announcement of his replacement, which is expected sooner than soon.

Feb 27, 2025

PREVIEW VERSION

For full access to Puck Inner Circle content, upgrade today.

Lauren Sherman

Hi, and welcome back to Line Sheet, live from Milan, where Sabato De Sarno wants you to know he is not slinking away. The former Gucci designer was out and about this week as anticipation builds about the impending announcement of his replacement, which is expected sooner than soon.

In today\xe2\x80\x99s Inner Circle issue, I explore why the search for Sabato\xe2\x80\x99s successor is so consequential to the future of Gucci and parentco Kering, but not in the way you might think. Up top, I have a near-instant report from the Prada show and notes on the Silvia Venturini Fendi rally\xe2\x80\x94and, for a special treat, Sarah Shapiro explains how Nuuly used its second-mover advantage to make the clothing rental business model work.

By the time you read this, I\xe2\x80\x99ll be heading home from a dinner in the Portrait Milano with retailer Antonia Giacinti and Khaite designer Catherine Holstein . (Khaite did a takeover of the Antonia store across the way.) My pre-Oscars party FOMO is growing, though. Tonight in West Hollywood, UTA\xe2\x80\x99s Dan Constable and Future Publishing\xe2\x80\x99s Hillary Kerr are hosting a dinner at Marvin for the fashion people stuck (?) on the West Coast this weekend. (They\xe2\x80\x99re calling it \xe2\x80\x9ccarbs before the carpet.\xe2\x80\x9d) I\xe2\x80\x99m especially sad to miss it, given that the event was partly inspired by my plea to the Academy to consult Dan next time they\xe2\x80\x99re picking a date for the Oscars. (We missed him at Prada today.) I expect a full report from those in attendance!

\xf0\x9f\x9a\xa8\xf0\x9f\x9a\xa8 Programming note : Tomorrow on Fashion People , I\xe2\x80\x99m joined by P.R. extraordinaire Lucien Pag\xc3\xa8s , who sold his business late last year to The Independents, a marketing, events, and comms conglomerate in the making. We talk about his exit, interning at Yves Saint Laurent in the 1990s, fashion in America versus Europe, being nice, and plenty more. Listen here and here .

\xf0\x9f\x9b\x8d\xef\xb8\x8f \xf0\x9f\x9b\x8d\xef\xb8\x8f For those of you with the Shoppies : This is a very un-Euro shopping rec during a week when I\xe2\x80\x99m contemplating Phoebe Philo suiting and Jermyn Street Scottish cashmere, but it feels urgent: Jessica Taft Langdon , the shoe designer and friend of Line Sheet, recently posted a gorgeous vintage handbag from\xe2\x80\xa6 Liz Claiborne. I have a deep appreciation for these bags, which were popular in the \xe2\x80\x9980s. (My parents bought me a knockoff version at Kids \xe2\x80\x9cR\xe2\x80\x9d Us.) The bags, of course, were a riff on the European luxury monograms of the time\xe2\x80\x94designed for a mass, American audience\xe2\x80\x94but Claiborne did an ingenious thing and created an original pyramid pattern that feels special now . Buy one here , here , or here .


Mentioned in this issue : Gucci, Sabato De Sarno , Kering, Francesca Bellettini , Silvia Venturini Fendi , Daniel Lee , Burberry, Prada, Saint Laurent, Raf Simons , Miuccia Prada , LVMH, Pierre-Emmanuel Angeloglou , Sarah Jessica Parker , Anna Wintour , Nuuly, Rent the Runway, Luke and Lucie Meier , Jil Sander, Parker Posey , and many more\xe2\x80\xa6

Three Things You Should Know\xe2\x80\xa6

  • Prada\xe2\x80\x99s black moment : Backstage at the Prada show, Raf Simons accidentally wrote the headline for plenty of reviews: \xe2\x80\x9cPrada\xe2\x80\x99s Anti-Quiet Luxury.\xe2\x80\x9d Yep, the Raf Simons uttered the dreaded empty phrase. \xe2\x80\x9cI know I\xe2\x80\x99m not allowed to say it, but I said it,\xe2\x80\x9d he added. \xe2\x80\x9cWe read what you write, and you all write about it!\xe2\x80\x9d

    I believe Simons was trying to say that he and co-creative director, Miuccia Prada , are opposed to...

UPGRADE TO THE INNER CIRCLE

Gucci Successorology & Burberry\xe2\x80\x99s Mini Revival

Even as Gucci prepares to announce a new creative director, the industry is reorienting away from star designers and runway buzz toward more sustainable strategies that put brands front and center.

Lauren Sherman

The biggest surprise this week so far was not that Luke and Lucie Meier , the designers of Jil Sander since 2017, were leaving the brand. That news, delivered less than an hour after the press release for their final collection dropped, had been widely reported weeks earlier. Reps for the fashion house\xe2\x80\x99s parent company, which is eyeing an I.P.O. next year, made it clear that this was a conscious uncoupling: no hard feelings, everyone was ready to move on, etcetera. Nor was I surprised by the Meiers\xe2\x80\x99 final collection, which leaned into the...

Puck founding partner Matt Belloni takes you inside the business of Hollywood, using exclusive reporting and insight to explain the backstories on everything from Marvel movies to the streaming wars.

Puck\xe2\x80\x99s daily art market email, anchored by industry expert Marion Maneker, offers unparalleled access to the mega-auctions and galleries, elite buyers and sellers, and the power players who run this opaque world. Wall Power also features Julie Brener Davich, a veteran of Christie\xe2\x80\x99s and Sotheby\xe2\x80\x99s, who provides unique insights into how the business really works.
